<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)) -->

# Container Image Tags Are Build Identifiers, Not Versions

A container image tag is a mutable label pointing at one image digest at
some moment in the registry. The kubelet treats a tag-only reference plus
the default `imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent` as "use whatever digest the
node has cached for that tag." Pushing a new build to an existing tag and
restarting Pods does **not** reliably ship the new build — nodes that
already cached the old digest never repull.

Treat each build as a distinct artifact and give it a distinct reference.

## The Rule

Every new build of an image gets a new tag, OR the workload pins the
image by digest and the digest is updated on every build. Pick one:

```yaml
# Option 1 — rotate the tag per build
image: ghcr.io/<owner>/<image>:0.1.0-20260524     # date-stamped
image: ghcr.io/<owner>/<image>:0.1.0-rev2         # build-counter
image: ghcr.io/<owner>/<image>:0.1.1              # bump the patch

# Option 2 — pin by digest (most rigorous)
image: ghcr.io/<owner>/<image>@sha256:<digest>
```

Argo CD / Helm / kustomize reconciles the new `image:` field, the Pod
template hash changes, the rollout starts, the kubelet sees an
unfamiliar reference and pulls it.

**Never** push a new build over an existing tag and rely on
`kubectl rollout restart` to pick it up. Restart alone does not
invalidate the kubelet cache.

## Why this matters

- `imagePullPolicy` defaults to `IfNotPresent` for any tag other than
  `:latest`. The kubelet skips the registry call when the tag is
  already present on the node, regardless of whether the registry now
  points that tag at a different digest.
- Multi-node clusters compound the problem: pods on nodes that pulled
  the tag earlier run the old digest; pods scheduled to nodes that
  never saw the tag pull the new one. The Deployment looks healthy and
  the behavior is split-brain by node.
- Cluster autoscaler-introduced new nodes inherit the same gap — any
  warmed copy of the tag (from this workload or any other) satisfies
  `IfNotPresent` for the new Pod.
- `kubectl rollout restart` rotates Pods through the same template
  hash. The new Pod's image reference is unchanged, so kubelet logic
  is unchanged. The cached digest wins.

## Concrete failure mode

setup-k3s deployed `ghcr.io/jewzaam/claude-quota-exporter:0.1.0` from a
build that predated a refactor commit. The operator rebuilt the image,
re-pushed `:0.1.0`, and waited for the rollout. In-cluster pods kept
running the old code because the cached `:0.1.0` digest satisfied
`IfNotPresent`. Acceptance criteria for the workload deploy failed
verification until the tag was rotated. Source:
<https://github.com/jewzaam/setup-k3s>.

## `imagePullPolicy: Always` is not the answer

Setting `imagePullPolicy: Always` forces a registry round-trip on every
Pod start. It does work around the cache problem, but it adds:

- Per-Pod-start latency (registry pull check, often the slowest step
  in cold start).
- Hard dependency on registry availability — a registry outage blocks
  every new Pod, including reschedules during a node drain.
- Exposure to registry rate limits (Docker Hub anonymous pulls cap
  visibly; GHCR has similar limits for unauthenticated traffic).

Use `Always` for development / dev-loop iteration where the tag really
is mutable on purpose. For production-grade workloads, rotate tags or
pin digests instead.

## When to apply

- Every image reference in every workload manifest the user authors.
- Every CI pipeline that pushes images — emit a unique tag per build
  (commit SHA, build number, or date stamp) and update the manifest
  to that tag.
- Charts and operators that ship a default `image.tag` — verify the
  chart maintainer rotates the tag per release, or pin a digest in
  the values override.

## When skipping is acceptable

- Local development against a single node where the operator
  explicitly wants `imagePullPolicy: Always` and accepts the cost.
- Immutable upstream images already pinned by digest (most stable
  Helm charts).

## Recognizing the failure mode

- A `kubectl describe pod` shows the expected `Image:` and `Image ID:`
  for the Pod, but the `Image ID:` (digest) is different from the
  digest currently associated with that tag in the registry.
- `crictl images` or `ctr images list` on the node shows the tag
  bound to a digest that no longer matches the registry.
- Two Pods of the same Deployment, scheduled to different nodes,
  exhibit different behavior — symptomatic of split-digest state
  across nodes.

## Provenance

Rule surfaced while verifying acceptance criteria for the
`claude-quota-exporter` workload deploy in
<https://github.com/jewzaam/setup-k3s>. Re-pushing `:0.1.0` after a
refactor did not propagate; tag rotation fixed it.
